Sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच ।
तं तथा कृपयाविष्टम् अश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम् ।
विषीदन्तम् इदं वाक्यम् उवाच मधुसूदनः ॥
English Translation: Sañjaya said: Seeing Arjuna overwhelmed with compassion, his eyes filled with tears and his mind full of grief, Lord Kṛṣṇa spoke the following words.
Inner Meaning: Arjuna’s emotional collapse sets the stage for spiritual teaching. True wisdom often begins when a person recognizes their confusion and limitation.

Sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
कुतस्त्वा कश्मलम् इदं विषमे समुपस्थितम् ।
अनार्यजुष्टम् अस्वर्ग्यम् अकीर्तिकरम् अर्जुन ॥
English Translation: The Supreme Lord said: My dear Arjuna, how has this weakness come upon you in this critical moment? It is not befitting a noble person, and it leads neither to honor nor to higher destiny.
Inner Meaning: Kṛṣṇa immediately challenges Arjuna’s despair. Spiritual growth begins when one confronts weakness rather than surrendering to it.

Sanskrit: क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते ।क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप ॥
English Translation: O son of Pṛthā, do not yield to this weakness. It does not suit you. Cast off this petty faintheartedness and arise, O conqueror of enemies.
Inner Meaning: Kṛṣṇa encourages courage and responsibility. Spiritual wisdom is not passive—it demands strength and action.

Sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच ।
कथं भीष्मम् अहं सङ्ख्ये द्रोणं च मधुसूदन ।
इषुभिः प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हाव् अरिसूदन ॥
English Translation: Arjuna said: O Kṛṣṇa, how can I fight against Bhīṣma and Droṇa in battle? They are worthy of my respect and worship.
Inner Meaning: Arjuna’s conflict deepens. His respect for his teachers and elders creates a moral dilemma between duty and emotional attachment.

Sanskrit: गुरून् अहत्वा हि महानुभावान् श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके । हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनिहैव भुञ्जीय भोगान् रुधिरप्रदिग्धान् ॥
English Translation: It would be better to live in this world by begging than to kill these great teachers. If we kill them for the sake of worldly gain, all the pleasures we enjoy will be stained with blood.
Inner Meaning: Arjuna fears that victory achieved through violence against loved ones will destroy moral purity.

Sanskrit: न चैतद् विद्मः कतरन्नो गरीयो यद्वा जयेम यदि वा नो जयेयुः । यानेव हत्वा न जिजीविषामस् तेऽवस्थिताः प्रमुखे धार्तराष्ट्राः ॥
English Translation: We do not know which is better—conquering them or being conquered by them. The sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra stand before us, and after killing them we would not even wish to live
Inner Meaning: Arjuna’s confusion intensifies. His moral uncertainty shows the complexity of ethical decisions in real life.

Sanskrit: कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभावः पृच्छामि त्वां धर्मसम्मूढचेताः । यच्छ्रेयः स्यान्निश्चितं ब्रूहि तन्मे शिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां प्रपन्नम् ॥
English Translation: My nature is overcome by weakness, and I am confused about my duty. I ask You to tell me clearly what is best. I am Your disciple and have surrendered to You—please instruct me.
Inner Meaning: This verse marks a turning point. Arjuna moves from argument to surrender, opening himself to genuine spiritual guidance.

Sanskrit: न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्यात् यच्छोकम् उच्छोषणम् इन्द्रियाणाम् । अवाप्य भूमावसपत्नम् ऋद्धं राज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम् ॥
English Translation: I cannot see anything that could remove this sorrow which is drying up my senses—even if I were to gain a prosperous kingdom on earth or sovereignty among the gods.
Inner Meaning: Material success cannot cure spiritual confusion. True peace comes only through deeper understanding.

Sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाच । एवम् उक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेशः परन्तप । न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दम् उक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह ॥
English Translation: Sañjaya said: Having spoken thus to Kṛṣṇa, Arjuna declared, “Govinda, I will not fight,” and fell silent.
Inner Meaning: Arjuna reaches complete emotional exhaustion. This silence prepares the ground for Kṛṣṇa’s deeper philosophical teaching.

Sanskrit:
तमुवाच हृषीकेशः प्रहसन्निव भारत ।
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वचः ॥
English Translation: O descendant of Bharata, at that moment Kṛṣṇa, smiling in the midst of the two armies, spoke the following words to the grief-stricken Arjuna.
Inner Meaning: Kṛṣṇa’s calm smile represents divine wisdom. Even in the midst of chaos and war, spiritual clarity remains peaceful and steady.

Sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
कुतस्त्वा कश्मलम् इदं विषमे समुपस्थितम् ।
अनार्यजुष्टम् अस्वर्ग्यम् अकीर्तिकरम् अर्जुन ॥
English Translation: The Supreme Lord said: While speaking learned words, you are grieving for what is not worthy of grief. The wise do not lament for the living nor for the dead.
Inner Meaning: True wisdom sees beyond temporary bodies. Grief arises when we identify only with the physical existence rather than the eternal soul.

Sanskrit:
न त्वेवाहं जातु नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः ।
न चैव न भविष्यामः सर्वे वयमतः परम् ॥
English Translation: Never was there a time when I did not exist, nor you, nor all these kings; nor in the future shall any of us cease to exist.
Inner Meaning: This verse introduces the idea of the eternal soul. Existence is continuous—birth and death are only transformations of the body.

Sanskrit:
देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा ।
तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति ॥
English Translation: Just as the embodied soul passes in this body from childhood to youth to old age, so also it passes into another body at death. A wise person is not confused by this.
Inner Meaning: Change of body is natural and constant. Death is simply another stage in the journey of the soul.

Sanskrit:
मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः ।
आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत ॥
English Translation: O son of Kuntī, the sensations of heat and cold, pleasure and pain arise from contact with the senses. They are temporary and come and go; therefore learn to tolerate them.
Inner Meaning: Life’s discomforts are temporary experiences. Spiritual maturity comes from patience and emotional stability.

Sanskrit:
यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ ।
समदुःखसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते ॥
English Translation: O best among men, the person who is not disturbed by happiness or distress and remains steady in both becomes eligible for liberation.
Inner Meaning: Inner balance is a sign of wisdom. Freedom comes when a person rises above emotional extremes.

Sanskrit:
नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सतः ।
उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः ॥
English Translation: The unreal has no lasting existence, and the real never ceases to be. The seers of truth have concluded this by studying the nature of both.
Inner Meaning: Material forms change and disappear, but the soul is eternal. Understanding this distinction is the foundation of spiritual knowledge.

Sanskrit:
अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ।
विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित्कर्तुमर्हति ॥
English Translation: Know that the soul which pervades the entire body is indestructible. No one can destroy that imperishable being.
Inner Meaning: The soul cannot be harmed by physical forces. Its existence is beyond the reach of material destruction.

Sanskrit:
अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ताः शरीरिणः ।
अनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत ॥
English Translation: The physical body is perishable, but the soul residing within it is eternal and indestructible. Therefore, arise and fight, O Arjuna.
Inner Meaning: Kṛṣṇa reminds Arjuna that the body is temporary. Understanding this allows one to perform duty without fear.

Sanskrit:
य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम् ।
उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते ॥
English Translation: One who thinks the soul kills or can be killed does not understand. The soul neither kills nor is killed.
Inner Meaning: Violence belongs to the physical world of bodies. The soul itself is beyond such actions.

Sanskrit:
न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचित्
नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः ।
अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो
न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥
English Translation: The soul is never born and never dies. It has always existed and will always exist. It is unborn, eternal, everlasting, and ancient. The soul is not destroyed when the body is destroyed.
Inner Meaning: This is one of the most important teachings of the Gītā: the soul is immortal. Death is only the end of the body, not the end of existence.

Sanskrit:
वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम् ।
कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम् ॥
English Translation: O Arjuna, one who knows the soul to be indestructible, eternal, unborn, and unchanging—how can such a person kill anyone or cause anyone to be killed?
Inner Meaning: When one understands the eternal nature of the soul, fear and confusion disappear. True knowledge transforms how we see life and death.

Sanskrit:
वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय
नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि ।
तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णान्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही ॥
English Translation: Just as a person discards worn-out clothes and puts on new ones, the soul discards old bodies and takes on new ones.
Inner Meaning: Death is not an end but a transition. The soul simply changes its physical covering.

Sanskrit:
नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः ।
न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः ॥
English Translation: Weapons cannot cut the soul, fire cannot burn it, water cannot wet it, and wind cannot dry it.
Inner Meaning: The soul is beyond all material elements. Nothing in the physical universe can destroy it.

Sanskrit:
अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च ।
नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः ॥
English Translation: The soul cannot be cut, burned, wetted, or dried. It is eternal, all-pervading, stable, immovable, and everlasting.
Inner Meaning: The soul’s true nature is constant and unchanging. Realizing this brings deep spiritual confidence.

Sanskrit:
अव्यक्तोऽयमचिन्त्योऽयमविकार्योऽयमुच्यते ।
तस्मादेवं विदित्वैनं नानुशोचितुमर्हसि ॥
English Translation: The soul is invisible, inconceivable, and unchangeable. Knowing this, you should not grieve for the body
Inner Meaning: Our grief arises from misunderstanding the nature of existence. Spiritual knowledge removes unnecessary sorrow.

Sanskrit:
अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम् ।
तथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैवं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
English Translation: Even if you think the soul is constantly born and constantly dies, still you should not lament, O mighty-armed Arjuna.
Inner Meaning: Kṛṣṇa argues logically: even from a purely material perspective, death is natural and inevitable..

Sanskrit:
जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च ।
तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
English Translation: For one who is born, death is certain; and for one who dies, birth is certain again. Therefore, you should not lament over the unavoidable.
Inner Meaning: The cycle of birth and death is a universal law. Understanding this helps one accept reality with maturity.

Sanskrit:
अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत ।
अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना ॥
English Translation: All beings are unmanifest before birth, manifest in the middle, and unmanifest again after death. Why then lament?
Inner Meaning: Life is just a brief visible phase between two unseen states. This perspective reduces fear of death.

Sanskrit:
आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेनम्
आश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्यः ।
आश्चर्यवच्चैनमन्यः शृणोति
श्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित् ॥
English Translation: Some see the soul as amazing, some describe it as amazing, some hear about it as amazing; yet even after hearing about it, few truly understand it.
Inner Meaning: The soul is a profound mystery. Intellectual knowledge alone is not enough—realization requires deep spiritual insight.

Sanskrit:
देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत ।
तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
English Translation: The soul dwelling in every body is eternal and cannot be killed. Therefore you should not grieve for any living being.
Inner Meaning: Understanding the immortality of the soul allows a person to rise above fear, sorrow, and confusion.

Sanskrit:
स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि ।
धर्म्याद्धि युद्धाच्छ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते ॥
English Translation: Considering your own duty as a warrior, you should not hesitate. For a warrior, there is no duty better than fighting for righteousness.
Inner Meaning: Considering your own duty as a warrior, you should not hesitate. For a warrior, there is no duty better than fighting for righteousness.

Sanskrit:
यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम् ।
सुखिनः क्षत्रियाः पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम् ॥
English Translation: O Arjuna, fortunate are the warriors who are given the opportunity to fight such a righteous battle, opening the gates of heaven.
Inner Meaning: O Arjuna, fortunate are the warriors who are given the opportunity to fight such a righteous battle, opening the gates of heaven.

Sanskrit:
अथ चेत्त्वमिमं धर्म्यं संग्रामं न करिष्यसि ।
ततः स्वधर्मं कीर्तिं च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥
English Translation: If you do not perform this righteous duty, you will lose your honor and incur sin.
Inner Meaning: Neglecting one’s rightful responsibility creates negative consequences both internally and externally.

Sanskrit:
अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम् ।
सम्भावितस्य चाकीर्तिर्मरणादतिरिच्यते ॥
English Translation: People will speak of your disgrace forever. For a respected person, dishonor is worse than death.
Inner Meaning: Reputation earned through righteousness carries great value. Losing integrity is more painful than physical defeat..

Sanskrit:
भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथाः ।
येषां च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम् ॥
English Translation: The great warriors will think you fled the battlefield out of fear, and those who once respected you will consider you insignificant.
Inner Meaning: Running away from responsibility damages self-respect and the respect of others.

Sanskrit:
अवाच्यवादांश्च बहून्वदिष्यन्ति तवाहिताः ।
निन्दन्तस्तव सामर्थ्यं ततो दुःखतरं नु किम् ॥
English Translation: Your enemies will speak many insulting words, mocking your ability. What could be more painful than that?
Inner Meaning: Avoiding duty invites criticism and regret. Courage protects dignity..

Sanskrit:
हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्गं जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम् ।
तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयः ॥
English Translation: If you die in battle, you will attain heaven; if you win, you will enjoy the kingdom on earth. Therefore stand up and fight with determination.
Inner Meaning: Kṛṣṇa encourages action without fear of outcome. Both success and failure can hold value when the intention is righteous.

Sanskrit:
सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ ।
ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥
English Translation: Treat pleasure and pain, gain and loss, victory and defeat alike, and then engage in battle. In this way you will not incur sin.
Inner Meaning: True spiritual action requires equanimity. When actions are free from attachment to results, they become pure.

Sanskrit:
एषा तेऽभिहिता सांख्ये बुद्धिर्योगे त्विमां शृणु ।
बुद्ध्या युक्तो यया पार्थ कर्मबन्धं प्रहास्यसि ॥
English Translation: So far I have explained this knowledge through philosophy. Now listen to the teaching of yoga, by which you can free yourself from the bondage of action.
Inner Meaning: Kṛṣṇa now transitions from philosophical knowledge to karma-yoga, the path of selfless action.

Sanskrit:
नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते ।
स्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात् ॥
English Translation: In this path there is no loss or negative result. Even a small amount of progress protects one from great fear.
Inner Meaning: Every step toward spiritual awareness has value. No sincere effort is ever wasted..

Sanskrit:
व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरुनन्दन ।
बहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्यवसायिनाम् ॥
English Translation: O descendant of the Kurus, those who are resolute in purpose have a focused intelligence, whereas the minds of the irresolute are scattered in many directions.
Inner Meaning: Spiritual growth requires clarity and determination. A distracted mind cannot achieve deep understanding.

Sanskrit:
यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चितः ।
वेदवादरताः पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिनः ॥
English Translation: Those with limited understanding become attached to the flowery language of the Vedas, claiming there is nothing beyond the pursuit of material rewards.
Inner Meaning: Kṛṣṇa warns against misinterpreting spiritual texts for material desires. True wisdom goes beyond ritualistic rewards.

Sanskrit:
कामात्मानः स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम् ।
क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति ॥
English Translation: Such people are driven by desires and seek heavenly pleasures. Their teachings focus on rituals meant to gain enjoyment and power.
Inner Meaning: When spirituality is driven by desire, it becomes a tool for pleasure rather than a path to liberation.

Sanskrit:
भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम् ।
व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिः समाधौ न विधीयते ॥
English Translation: Those whose minds are attached to pleasure and power cannot develop the steady determination required for spiritual realization.
Inner Meaning: Attachment to luxury and status prevents inner stability and deeper awareness.

Sanskrit:
त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन ।
निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान् ॥
English Translation: The Vedas deal with the three qualities of nature. Rise above these qualities, O Arjuna. Be free from dualities and remain steady in the self.
Inner Meaning: Spiritual progress requires transcending the influences of nature—such as desire, fear, and attachment.

Sanskrit:
यावानर्थ उदपाने सर्वतः संप्लुतोदके ।
तावान्सर्वेषु वेदेषु ब्राह्मणस्य विजानतः ॥
English Translation: Just as a small well becomes unnecessary when there is abundant water everywhere, similarly one who understands truth sees the purpose of all scriptures fulfilled.
Inner Meaning: Direct realization of truth surpasses theoretical knowledge.

Sanskrit:
कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन ।
मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते संगोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ॥
English Translation: You have the right to perform your duty, but never to the results of your actions. Do not be motivated by the fruits of action, nor become attached to inaction.
Inner Meaning: This verse defines karma-yoga. Act sincerely, but detach from the outcome.

Sanskrit:
योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि संगं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय ।
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते ॥
English Translation: Perform your duties with balance of mind, abandoning attachment to success or failure. Such equanimity is called yoga.
Inner Meaning: True yoga is not just meditation—it is the ability to remain balanced in all situations.

Sanskrit:
दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय ।
बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणाः फलहेतवः ॥
English Translation: Action performed for selfish rewards is far inferior to action performed with spiritual wisdom. Seek refuge in such wisdom.
Inner Meaning: When actions are guided by wisdom instead of desire, they become liberating..

Sanskrit:
बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते ।
तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योगः कर्मसु कौशलम् ॥
English Translation: A person acting with spiritual wisdom frees themselves from both good and bad reactions. Therefore engage in yoga, which is skill in action.
Inner Meaning: Yoga means acting with awareness, balance, and detachment. This transforms ordinary work into spiritual practice.

Sanskrit:
कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः ।
जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम् ॥
English Translation: The wise, acting with spiritual understanding and abandoning attachment to the fruits of their actions, become free from the cycle of birth and death and attain a state beyond all suffering.
Inner Meaning: When actions are performed selflessly, they no longer bind the soul. Freedom comes through detachment from outcomes.

Sanskrit:
यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति ।
तदा गन्तासि निर्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च ॥
English Translation: When your intelligence passes beyond the confusion of illusion, you will become indifferent to what has been heard and what is yet to be heard.
Inner Meaning: True wisdom removes confusion and frees the mind from endless speculation.

Sanskrit:
श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला ।
समाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि ॥
English Translation: When your mind becomes steady and undisturbed by conflicting teachings, and remains firmly fixed in spiritual realization, you will attain true yoga.
Inner Meaning: Yoga is achieved when the mind becomes stable and focused on truth.

Sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच ।
स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव ।
स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम् ॥
English Translation: Arjuna said: O Kṛṣṇa, what are the characteristics of a person whose wisdom is steady and who is absorbed in meditation? How does such a person speak, sit, and walk?
Inner Meaning: Arjuna now asks about the qualities of an enlightened person.

Sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
प्रजहाति यदा कामान् सर्वान् पार्थ मनोगतान् ।
आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते ॥
English Translation: The Supreme Lord said: When a person gives up all desires arising from the mind and finds satisfaction in the self alone, that person is called one whose wisdom is steady.
Inner Meaning: A stable mind arises when happiness comes from within rather than external objects.

Sanskrit:
दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः ।
वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते ॥
English Translation: One whose mind is undisturbed in sorrow, free from craving in happiness, and free from attachment, fear, and anger is called a sage of steady wisdom.
Inner Meaning: Emotional balance is a key sign of spiritual maturity.

Sanskrit:
यः सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम् ।
नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
English Translation: One who is not attached to anything and neither rejoices nor hates when encountering good or bad fortune has steady wisdom.
Inner Meaning: A wise person remains balanced regardless of external circumstances.

Sanskrit:
यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः ।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
English Translation: When a person withdraws their senses from sense objects, just as a tortoise withdraws its limbs, their wisdom becomes steady.
Inner Meaning: Control over the senses is essential for inner stability.

Sanskrit:
विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः ।
रसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते ॥
English Translation: Although one may refrain from sense pleasures, the taste for them remains. But upon experiencing something higher, even that desire disappears.
Inner Meaning: Higher spiritual joy naturally replaces lower desires.

Sanskrit:
यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः ।
इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः ॥
English Translation: Even for a wise person striving for self-control, the restless senses can forcibly carry away the mind.
Inner Meaning: Even for a wise person striving for self-control, the restless senses can forcibly carry away the mind.

Sanskrit:
तानि सर्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्परः ।
वशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
English Translation: One who restrains all the senses and remains devoted to Me with discipline—such a person has steady wisdom.
Inner Meaning: Spiritual stability comes through discipline and focusing the mind on a higher purpose.

Sanskrit:
ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते ।
सङ्गात्संजायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते ॥
English Translation: When a person dwells on sense objects, attachment develops. From attachment arises desire, and from desire comes anger.
Inner Meaning: Attachment is the starting point of inner disturbance.

Sanskrit:
क्रोधाद्भवति सम्मोहः सम्मोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः ।
स्मृतिभ्रंशाद्बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति ॥
English Translation: From anger comes delusion; from delusion, loss of memory; from loss of memory, destruction of intelligence; and from destruction of intelligence, one falls into ruin.
Inner Meaning: Uncontrolled emotions gradually destroy clarity and wisdom.

Sanskrit:
रागद्वेषवियुक्तैस्तु विषयानिन्द्रियैश्चरन् ।
आत्मवश्यैर्विधेयात्मा प्रसादमधिगच्छति ॥
English Translation: One who controls the senses and remains free from attachment and hatred attains inner peace.
Inner Meaning: Peace arises when the mind is balanced and disciplined.

Sanskrit:
प्रसादे सर्वदुःखानां हानिरस्योपजायते ।
प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याशु बुद्धिः पर्यवतिष्ठते ॥
English Translation: For one who has attained peace of mind, all sorrows disappear. With a calm mind, intelligence quickly becomes steady.
Inner Meaning: Inner peace is the foundation of clear thinking.

Sanskrit:
नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना ।
न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम् ॥
English Translation: For one without self-discipline there is no wisdom, and without wisdom there is no meditation. Without meditation there is no peace, and without peace how can there be happiness?
Inner Meaning: Happiness depends on inner discipline and mental stability.

Sanskrit:
इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते ।
तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि ॥
English Translation: When the mind follows the wandering senses, it carries away a person’s intelligence just as a strong wind carries away a boat on water.
Inner Meaning: Without control of the senses, the mind becomes unstable.

Sanskrit:
तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वशः ।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
English Translation: Therefore, O mighty-armed Arjuna, one whose senses are completely restrained from their objects has steady wisdom.
Inner Meaning: Self-mastery leads to clarity and strength.

Sanskrit:
या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी ।
यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः ॥
English Translation: What is night for all beings is the time of awakening for the disciplined person; what others consider awakening is night for the sag
Inner Meaning: A wise person sees reality differently from the materialistic world.

Sanskrit:
आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं
समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत् ।
तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे
स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी ॥
English Translation: Just as rivers flow into the ocean, which remains steady and undisturbed, similarly the person who is not disturbed by desires attains peace.
Inner Meaning: Peace comes from inner completeness, not from fulfilling desires.

Sanskrit:
विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः ।
निर्ममो निरहंकारः स शान्तिमधिगच्छति ॥
English Translation: One who gives up all desires, lives free from possessiveness and ego, and moves without craving attains true peace.
Inner Meaning: Freedom from ego and attachment leads to lasting tranquility.

Sanskrit:
एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिः पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति ।
स्थित्वास्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति ॥
English Translation: O Arjuna, this is the state of spiritual realization. Once attained, a person is never again deluded. Even at the moment of death, one who remains in this state attains liberation..
Inner Meaning: This verse concludes the chapter by describing the ultimate goal—stable spiritual awareness leading to liberation.
